description: Founder-mode risk policy. Tighter defaults because no human signs paper->live. Edit before going live.
notes: This file lives in the risk-policy service, NOT on the agent filesystem.
Agents get a read-only check endpoint. They cannot edit this file.
Every change to this file requires the founder's signature + a deliberate deploy.
Founder-mode amplifies the risk of removing humans from paper->live. Defaults are tighter.
firm_kill_switch_monthly_pct: if firm-level monthly drawdown breaches this, halt ALL trading and page the founder.
fail_mode=deny: the Execution Engineer MUST halt on any ambiguous signal — non-2xx risk check, risk-service timeout, stale quotes, broker disconnect. Never fail open.
risk_service_timeout_seconds: if /risk/check takes longer than this, treat as deny and halt.
max_quote_age_seconds: reject any order whose backing quote is older than this.
on_broker_disconnect: when IB Gateway / venue adapter disconnects, flatten open positions and halt until a human re-enables trading.

drawdown_reference=high_water_mark_excluding_deposits: drawdown is measured against peak equity with deposits/withdrawals netted out. Without this, adding a tranche silently resets the denominator and the kill switch becomes unreachable.

cold_start_multiplier_by_day: when a strategy is first promoted paper->live, size is multiplied by the entry for its age in live-days. Day 0-29 = 0.25x, 30-59 = 0.5x, 60+ = 1.0x. Paper Sharpe 1.0 on 30 trades has a wide confidence interval; size the uncertainty.

HWM state lives at /etc/hwm_state.json on the risk host, root/risk owned. The Accountant POSTs equity snapshots daily to /hwm/equity; tranches and withdrawals POST to /hwm/deposit so the denominator excludes them.
